content.text: Okay, what is left for week 5 (including open issues)

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- orchestration-abstraction-guard: Specialized reviewer that audits backend code for provider-specific branching that bypasses the OrchestrationProvider abstraction. Use this proactively when reviewing any PR that touches backend/app/orchestration/, backend/app/services/, or backend/app/api/ — especially any code path handling ADF or Airflow events, trigger bindings, or pipeline_runs. Also invoke when the user asks "does this go through the abstraction?" or similar. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
23:| **Current week** | **Week 5 of 8 (Execution engine + scheduling)** — in progress (17/19); Weeks 1–4 complete (26/26) |

Remaining: recent-runs table. Weeks 1–4 exit gates met — see [CLAUDE.md §13](../CLAUDE.md). Live warehouse/file run remains the deferred Week-1 smoke. |
27:| **Next milestone** | **Week 5 in progress (18/19)** — async-execution backend complete: `error` status (#297), flat-file batch + `skip` (#298), UC run-path coverage (#299), run progress API (#301), cancel run + #227 dedup (#302), `GET /orchestration/pipelines` (#305), gap-recovery beat (#307), scheduling backend (A7), retention sweep (A8) all shipped on top of the early-credit (Celery runner PR 4, worker dispatch #146, ADF/Airflow polling #171, `trigger_bindings` #172, target+dispatch #215, manual run + read API PR-C0b, Suite Triggers UI #216); plus the **live run-progress UI (A3)** and **scheduled-runs UI (A6)** on the frontend. **Remaining:** execution UI (recent-runs). |
87:- [ ] 🟡 Upsert pipeline run status into `pipeline_runs`; correlate with suite run — idempotent upsert (PR 7) + **trigger-on-success skeleton** (PR 8): a `succeeded` run matching enabled `trigger_bindings` creates queued `Run` rows (`triggered_by="<provider>:<pipeline>:<run_id>"`, idempotent on replay); failures never trigger (ADR 0004). **`run_suite` dispatch ungated** in Week 5 ([#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215), PR-C0a — `Suite.target` + `run_target` resolver; `_trigger_suites` now `run_suite.delay`s each created run); `trigger_bindings` CRUD shipped ([#172](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/190)) — its UI is Week 5 ([#216](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/216)). `list_recent_runs` + 10-min polling beat shipped ([#171](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/189)).
106:**Week 2 total: 15 / 19** _(ADF webhook receiver: endpoint+auth, payload parse, secret config, REST `fetch_run_detail` enrichment; upsert+correlate 🟡 — trigger-on-success skeleton landed, run_suite dispatch gated to Week 3; polling → Week 5. **Airflow group complete (3/3)** — HMAC receiver + connection adapter + DAG callback snippet, so the producer↔receiver loop is closed end-to-end. All six connection types now have adapters: Snowflake + ADF + Airflow + ADLS Gen2 + S3 + Unity Catalog. Remaining W2 tail: connection re-auth endpoint + `secret_ref` nullability note; the rest are Week-3/5 GX run-path tasks)_
118:- [x] ✅ API: check dry-run endpoint — validate against live data, return preview result — `POST /suites/{id}/checks/dryrun` (`dryrun_service`): runs **one ad-hoc check** against the suite's connection synchronously and returns a preview (severity `status` + `metric_value` + sanitized `observed/expected`) **without persisting** any Run/Result. Reuses the severity derivation (ADR 0005/0016) + JSON sanitiser. `require_permission` **edit** (authoring); table passed in the body (checks don't carry a target table yet). v1 → 422: non-`expectation` kind, non-Snowflake connection (runner dispatch generalises Week 5); execution failure → 502 (adapter exception never echoed). No `sample_failures` in the preview (PII; follow-up). 7 TestClient tests (mocked runner); `dryrun_service` + `checks.py` 100%
134:- [x] ✅ Column profiler endpoint (Snowflake) — nulls, distinct count, min / max, top values — `profile_service` + `POST /suites/{id}/profile` (require_permission **edit**, suite-scoped so the connection is access-gated). Reads-only, persists nothing: one aggregate query (row count + null/distinct/min/max per column) + one top-N-values query per column, then `assemble_profile`. **SQL-injection-safe** — queries are built with the **SQLAlchemy Core expression language** (`select`/`table`/`column`, dialect-quoted) so there's no raw-string SQL sink (no S608/B608/CodeQL `py/sql-injection`); identifiers are additionally allowlist-validated (`validate_identifier`, strict `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_$]*$`) as defence-in-depth + a clean early 422, and `top_n` is `int()`-coerced. v1 → 422: non-Snowflake type (dispatch generalises Week 5), bad identifier, no schema; execution failure → 502 (adapter exception never echoed). min/max/top-values NaN-sanitised. 28 tests (22 pure: identifier allowlist incl. injection strings, compiled-SQL builders, assembly, div-by-zero, NaN; 6 endpoint via fake conn: stats, injection-422, unsupported-type-422, 502, edit-gated, no-schema-422); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 90% (live `_open_connection` is the deferred warehouse seam)
197:## Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling
199:**Exit gate:** Async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational.
206:- [x] ✅ Run progress API — poll endpoint returning per-check live status — `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (suite-scoped): run lifecycle status + per-check resolution (status or null=pending) + a status histogram, the compact shape the live-progress UI polls. DB-driven (worker writes status + result rows); GX's atomic batch means per-check rows land at completion, so this reports lifecycle + final resolution (documented). **[PR #301](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/301)** (A1)
208:- [x] ✅ Run history retention policy — configurable purge of results older than N days — **PII-minimisation sweep** (not a row delete: ADR 0012 wants `metric_value` trends to outlive samples). Daily Celery-beat `purge_sample_failures` → `run_service.purge_expired_sample_failures` scrubs `sample_failures` (the only potentially-PII result column) to SQL NULL + stamps `sample_failures_purged_at` on results older than the configurable `sample_failures_retention_days` (default 30). Honoured the pre-built seam (the column + setting + the three "retention sweep" comments existed with no writer). Targets only real sample *objects* via `jsonb_typeof` (the JSONB column stores Python `None` as JSON `'null'`, so `IS NOT NULL` over-matches every passing/errored row); naturally idempotent (scrubbed → SQL NULL → excluded); `retention_days <= 0` disables. 6 DB-backed sweep tests + 1 task-wiring unit test. **(A8)**
211:- [x] ✅ **Scheduling backend** — cron-driven suite runs make "scheduling operational" (the backend half of the Execution-UI scheduled-runs table). New `schedules` table (migration `f7a8b9c0d1e2`, additive; cascade-deleted with the suite) holding a 5-field `cron` + IANA `timezone` (DST-aware) + precomputed `next_run_at` (indexed) per suite. `services/cron.py` wraps `croniter` + `zoneinfo` (validate cron/tz → 422; **no-backfill** `next_fire` = next fire strictly after the base, so a downtime gap fires once, not per missed slot). `schedule_service` CRUD mirrors `trigger_binding_service` (suite-permission-gated: `edit` to mutate, `view` to read; `next_run_at` recomputed on cadence-change / re-enable). `api/v1/schedules.py` (POST/GET/GET{id}/PATCH/DELETE). New Celery-beat tick `dispatch_due_schedules` (60s) scans `enabled, next_run_at<=now` with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (no double-fire across overlapping ticks), resolves the suite target up front (invalid → skip + advance, never a queued→failed run), queues a `Run` (`triggered_by=schedule:<id>`) + dispatches via the shared `run_dispatch` (broker-fail → `mark_dispatch_failed` #227). `cron.validate_cron` probes a fire so an impossible-calendar cron (Feb 30 — `croniter.is_valid` passes it) is a clean 422, not a 500-on-create / crashed dispatcher tick; the dispatcher self-disables such a row instead of hot-looping. 37 tests (cron units incl. DST spring-forward/fall-back + no-backfill + impossible-date; DB-backed CRUD/authz/422 API; dispatcher due/future/disabled/invalid-target/invalid-cron/no-backfill/broker-fail). **(A7)**
223:### Execution UI (5 tasks — 4/5 ✅)
225:- [x] ✅ Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button — `LiveRunProgress` drawer (`components/runs/`): opens on the run id returned by the manual **Run** button (#245) instead of bouncing to `/results`, and polls `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (#301) on a self-scheduling timeout (not `setInterval` — no overlapping fetches) until the run is terminal (`succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled`), then stops. Shows the run-status tag + a completed/total `Progress` bar + a per-check list (a `Spin`+"pending" while a check's `status` is null, its severity `Tag` once resolved). An editor (`canRun`) gets a **Cancel** button (`POST /runs/{id}/cancel` #302) on a non-terminal run; cancel reflects the terminal state immediately + the next poll confirms it. On terminal, a "View full results →" link to `/results`. Body keyed by run id so opening a new run restarts polling; `pollMs` is injectable for deterministic tests. 7 component tests (pending/resolved render, poll-until-terminal-then-stop, cancel happy path, cancel hidden when terminal / non-editor, fetch-error alert). **(A3)**
226:- [x] ✅ Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules — `SchedulesPanel` (`components/suites/`): suite-detail card over the A7 schedules CRUD (#314) — an antd table (cron · timezone · next run · last run · status · delete) + an add form (cron `Input` + IANA-timezone `Select`, default UTC). **Pause/resume** is the `enabled` toggle (`PATCH /schedules/{id}`); **delete** is `DELETE /schedules/{id}`; **create** is `POST /schedules`. Edit-gated (`canManage` = `canRun`): viewers see a read-only `enabled`/`paused` tag, no controls — matching the backend gate. Timezone list comes from `Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone')` (no hardcoded table; UTC-first). Cron/timezone validated server-side → 422 surfaced inline. `api/schedules.ts` + 6 component tests (list, empty, create-with-UTC-default, pause-toggle, delete, viewer-read-only). **(A6)**
230:**Week 5 total: 18 / 19** _(early credit: Celery task runner in PR 4; worker runner-dispatch #146; ADF + Airflow 10-min polling #171; plus `trigger_bindings` CRUD #172 out-of-band; check target-table + dispatch ungate #215 (PR-C0a); manual run trigger + run/result read API (PR-C0b); **Suite Triggers UI #216 (PR #247)**. **This stretch (Phase 1–4):** `error` status #297, flat-file batch + `skip` #298, UC run-path coverage #299, run progress API #301, cancel run + #227 #302, `GET /orchestration/pipelines` #305, gap-recovery beat #307, **scheduling backend (A7)**, **live run-progress UI (A3)**, **scheduled-runs UI (A6)**, **run-history retention (A8)**, **run-now panel**. +3 tasks re-tracked here: check target-table #215 (slipped from W3), Suite Triggers UI #216, and the run-enablement read API (PR-C0b); +1 added: the scheduling backend (A7), the backend the roadmap's scheduled-runs UI task implies. The **manual Run button** (PR #245) + the **run-target editor** (PR #246) also shipped earlier this week. **Remaining (1):** recent-runs audit table)_
256:- [ ] ⬜ Alert dedup — fire on first failure only, not on every subsequent scheduled run
265:- [ ] ⬜ **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard page** (`/dashboard`, `DashboardScreen`) — the new landing screen that **hosts the existing Week-6 dashboard widgets** (health-score stat cards, 7-day quality-trend chart, per-suite performance bars, recent-runs table). This is the **page home** for the "Health score stat cards", "Per-suite pass/fail bars" and "Per-check historical trend chart" tasks above — build them here rather than on `/results`. **Prereq:** a chart-library decision (antd ships no charts → `@ant-design/charts` or `recharts`; runs through `pnpm audit` + bundle-size gate → ADR-light note). Render only backend-backed KPIs (see the W7 *KPI honesty pass*). _Design ref: `templates/app/DashboardScreen.jsx`; components `data/MetricCard.jsx`, `data/Card.jsx`, `feedback/ProgressBar.jsx`, `data/DataTable.jsx`; readme §"Templates" (Enhanced Monitoring — KPIs · Quality Trends · Suite Performance · Recent Runs)._
345:- [ ] 🟡 Execution service — `run_suite` dispatch + `run_service.execute_run` + GX adapter + NaN sanitizer tested early ([PR 4b.1](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/77) + [PR 4c-i](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/78) + [PR 4c-ii](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/79)); progress polling / cancel / retention purge pending
347:- [ ] 🟡 ADF service — parse + upsert + `fetch_run_detail` + enrichment + trigger-on-success covered (`AdfProvider` parse/fetch unit tests incl. ARM mapping/http-error; `orchestration_service` DB tests incl. replay idempotency, enrichment fail-soft, trigger gating — modules 100%) — PR 7 + PR 8; `list_recent_runs` polling + gap-recovery dedup pending Week 5

206:- [x] ✅ Run progress API — poll endpoint returning per-check live status — `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (suite-scoped): run lifecycle status + per-check resolution (status or null=pending) + a status histogram, the compact shape the live-progress UI polls. DB-driven (worker writes status + result rows); GX's atomic batch means per-check rows land at completion, so this reports lifecycle + final resolution (documented). **[PR #301](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/301)** (A1)
208:- [x] ✅ Run history retention policy — configurable purge of results older than N days — **PII-minimisation sweep** (not a row delete: ADR 0012 wants `metric_value` trends to outlive samples). Daily Celery-beat `purge_sample_failures` → `run_service.purge_expired_sample_failures` scrubs `sample_failures` (the only potentially-PII result column) to SQL NULL + stamps `sample_failures_purged_at` on results older than the configurable `sample_failures_retention_days` (default 30). Honoured the pre-built seam (the column + setting + the three "retention sweep" comments existed with no writer). Targets only real sample *objects* via `jsonb_typeof` (the JSONB column stores Python `None` as JSON `'null'`, so `IS NOT NULL` over-matches every passing/errored row); naturally idempotent (scrubbed → SQL NULL → excluded); `retention_days <= 0` disables. 6 DB-backed sweep tests + 1 task-wiring unit test. **(A8)**
211:- [x] ✅ **Scheduling backend** — cron-driven suite runs make "scheduling operational" (the backend half of the Execution-UI scheduled-runs table). New `schedules` table (migration `f7a8b9c0d1e2`, additive; cascade-deleted with the suite) holding a 5-field `cron` + IANA `timezone` (DST-aware) + precomputed `next_run_at` (indexed) per suite. `services/cron.py` wraps `croniter` + `zoneinfo` (validate cron/tz → 422; **no-backfill** `next_fire` = next fire strictly after the base, so a downtime gap fires once, not per missed slot). `schedule_service` CRUD mirrors `trigger_binding_service` (suite-permission-gated: `edit` to mutate, `view` to read; `next_run_at` recomputed on cadence-change / re-enable). `api/v1/schedules.py` (POST/GET/GET{id}/PATCH/DELETE). New Celery-beat tick `dispatch_due_schedules` (60s) scans `enabled, next_run_at<=now` with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (no double-fire across overlapping ticks), resolves the suite target up front (invalid → skip + advance, never a queued→failed run), queues a `Run` (`triggered_by=schedule:<id>`) + dispatches via the shared `run_dispatch` (broker-fail → `mark_dispatch_failed` #227). `cron.validate_cron` probes a fire so an impossible-calendar cron (Feb 30 — `croniter.is_valid` passes it) is a clean 422, not a 500-on-create / crashed dispatcher tick; the dispatcher self-disables such a row instead of hot-looping. 37 tests (cron units incl. DST spring-forward/fall-back + no-backfill + impossible-date; DB-backed CRUD/authz/422 API; dispatcher due/future/disabled/invalid-target/invalid-cron/no-backfill/broker-fail). **(A7)**
223:### Execution UI (5 tasks — 4/5 ✅)
225:- [x] ✅ Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button — `LiveRunProgress` drawer (`components/runs/`): opens on the run id returned by the manual **Run** button (#245) instead of bouncing to `/results`, and polls `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (#301) on a self-scheduling timeout (not `setInterval` — no overlapping fetches) until the run is terminal (`succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled`), then stops. Shows the run-status tag + a completed/total `Progress` bar + a per-check list (a `Spin`+"pending" while a check's `status` is null, its severity `Tag` once resolved). An editor (`canRun`) gets a **Cancel** button (`POST /runs/{id}/cancel` #302) on a non-terminal run; cancel reflects the terminal state immediately + the next poll confirms it. On terminal, a "View full results →" link to `/results`. Body keyed by run id so opening a new run restarts polling; `pollMs` is injectable for deterministic tests. 7 component tests (pending/resolved render, poll-until-terminal-then-stop, cancel happy path, cancel hidden when terminal / non-editor, fetch-error alert). **(A3)**
226:- [x] ✅ Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules — `SchedulesPanel` (`components/suites/`): suite-detail card over the A7 schedules CRUD (#314) — an antd table (cron · timezone · next run · last run · status · delete) + an add form (cron `Input` + IANA-timezone `Select`, default UTC). **Pause/resume** is the `enabled` toggle (`PATCH /schedules/{id}`); **delete** is `DELETE /schedules/{id}`; **create** is `POST /schedules`. Edit-gated (`canManage` = `canRun`): viewers see a read-only `enabled`/`paused` tag, no controls — matching the backend gate. Timezone list comes from `Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone')` (no hardcoded table; UTC-first). Cron/timezone validated server-side → 422 surfaced inline. `api/schedules.ts` + 6 component tests (list, empty, create-with-UTC-default, pause-toggle, delete, viewer-read-only). **(A6)**
230:**Week 5 total: 18 / 19** _(early credit: Celery task runner in PR 4; worker runner-dispatch #146; ADF + Airflow 10-min polling #171; plus `trigger_bindings` CRUD #172 out-of-band; check target-table + dispatch ungate #215 (PR-C0a); manual run trigger + run/result read API (PR-C0b); **Suite Triggers UI #216 (PR #247)**. **This stretch (Phase 1–4):** `error` status #297, flat-file batch + `skip` #298, UC run-path coverage #299, run progress API #301, cancel run + #227 #302, `GET /orchestration/pipelines` #305, gap-recovery beat #307, **scheduling backend (A7)**, **live run-progress UI (A3)**, **scheduled-runs UI (A6)**, **run-history retention (A8)**, **run-now panel**. +3 tasks re-tracked here: check target-table #215 (slipped from W3), Suite Triggers UI #216, and the run-enablement read API (PR-C0b); +1 added: the scheduling backend (A7), the backend the roadmap's scheduled-runs UI task implies. The **manual Run button** (PR #245) + the **run-target editor** (PR #246) also shipped earlier this week. **Remaining (1):** recent-runs audit table)_
256:- [ ] ⬜ Alert dedup — fire on first failure only, not on every subsequent scheduled run
265:- [ ] ⬜ **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard page** (`/dashboard`, `DashboardScreen`) — the new landing screen that **hosts the existing Week-6 dashboard widgets** (health-score stat cards, 7-day quality-trend chart, per-suite performance bars, recent-runs table). This is the **page home** for the "Health score stat cards", "Per-suite pass/fail bars" and "Per-check historical trend chart" tasks above — build them here rather than on `/results`. **Prereq:** a chart-library decision (antd ships no charts → `@ant-design/charts` or `recharts`; runs through `pnpm audit` + bundle-size gate → ADR-light note). Render only backend-backed KPIs (see the W7 *KPI honesty pass*). _Design ref: `templates/app/DashboardScreen.jsx`; components `data/MetricCard.jsx`, `data/Card.jsx`, `feedback/ProgressBar.jsx`, `data/DataTable.jsx`; readme §"Templates" (Enhanced Monitoring — KPIs · Quality Trends · Suite Performance · Recent Runs)._
345:- [ ] 🟡 Execution service — `run_suite` dispatch + `run_service.execute_run` + GX adapter + NaN sanitizer tested early ([PR 4b.1](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/77) + [PR 4c-i](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/78) + [PR 4c-ii](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/79)); progress polling / cancel / retention purge pending
347:- [ ] 🟡 ADF service — parse + upsert + `fetch_run_detail` + enrichment + trigger-on-success covered (`AdfProvider` parse/fetch unit tests incl. ARM mapping/http-error; `orchestration_service` DB tests incl. replay idempotency, enrichment fail-soft, trigger gating — modules 100%) — PR 7 + PR 8; `list_recent_runs` polling + gap-recovery dedup pending Week 5

## Week 5 — what's left

Per [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md#L230), Week 5 is **18/19 tasks done**. Everything substantive (run paths across all datasources, `error`/`skip` statuses, progress/cancel APIs, scheduling backend A7, retention sweep A8, live run-progress UI A3, scheduled-runs UI A6, run-now panel) has shipped.

### Remaining roadmap task (1)
- **Recent-runs audit table** — the last of the 5 Execution-UI tasks ([progress.md:223](docs/progress.md#L223)). This is the only thing standing between you and the Week-5 exit gate ("async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational").
  - ⚠️ Note the overlap with Week 6: the **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** ([progress.md:265](docs/progress.md#L265)) is slated to *host* a recent-runs table on `/dashboard`. Worth deciding whether to build the W5 recent-runs table standalone (e.g. on `/results` or suite detail) or fold it into the W6 dashboard to avoid building it twice.

### Open issues tagged to the Week 5 milestone (3)
| # | Title | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| [#147](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/147) | Profiler / flat-file cleanup — timeout drift, duplicated format+stat logic, N+1 profiler queries | Carried since W3 |
| [#317](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/317) | Move `barStatus()` into `resultsFormat.ts` next to `RUN_STATUS_COLORS` | Small frontend tidy |
| [#174](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/174) | Week 5 tracking epic | Closes when the week closes |

### Deferred-but-related (filed to Backlog, not blocking the gate)
These came out of Week-5 work but were intentionally pushed post-v1 — flagging so nothing's lost:
- [#308](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/308) — double-trigger race in `_trigger_suites` (non-atomic dedup, no unique constraint)
- [#318](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/318) — run progress is binary 0%→100% (GX atomic batch)
- [#306](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/306) — orchestration read endpoints silently 200 `[]` on a bad provider/env param
- [#322](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/322) — flaky `LiveRunProgress` test
- [#323](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/323) — index/batch the retention sweep for large tables
- [#326](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/326) — `RunNowPanel` redundant guard
- [#309](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/309) — reaper for runs stuck queued/running (tagged **Week 7**)

**Bottom line:** one feature task (recent-runs table) closes the Week-5 gate; #147 and #317 are cleanups you could fold in or push. Want me to start on the recent-runs table — and if so, standalone or as part of the W6 dashboard?

content.content:
## Profiler / flat-file cleanup: timeout drift, duplicated format+stat logic, N+1 profiler queries

Low-priority cleanup items surfaced during the full `/code-review` of `58193cd..HEAD` (column profiler + check runners, #137–#144). Grouped here; none is a correctness bug. The correctness/robustness findings are tracked separately in #145, and the dispatch/registry gap in #146.

### 1. S3 read-timeout drift (30s → 60s)
When the flat-file download was extracted into `flatfile.py`, the S3 read timeout changed from the profiler's original 30s (`profile_service._NETWORK_TIMEOUT`) to [`_READ_TIMEOUT = 60`](backend/app/datasources/flatfile.py#L38). More lenient than before; a hung S3 read now ties up a worker thread twice as long. Confirm 60s is intentional (full-file reads may legitimately need longer than a profiler sample) vs accidental drift, and align the two constants.

### 2. `_SUPPORTED_FORMATS` duplicates `format_from_path`
[`infer_file_format`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L333) calls `flatfile.format_from_path(path)` then checks membership in [`_SUPPORTED_FORMATS = {"csv","parquet"}`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L62). But `format_from_path` already only ever returns `csv`/`parquet`/`None`, so the supported-format set is expressed twice. Add JSON to `format_from_path` and the profiler silently still rejects it. Derive the supported set from `format_from_path`, or drop the redundant check.

### 3. Duplicated stat contract: SQL vs pandas
The per-column statistic definitions (null_fraction = nulls/row_count with the row_count==0 guard, distinct excludes nulls, top_values = non-null desc by count) are implemented twice with no shared code — once in SQL via [`assemble_profile`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L195) and once in pandas via [`profile_dataframe`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L362). A definitional change must be made in both or the two datasource families disagree for the same logical data. (Hard to fully DRY across SQL vs pandas, but the *contract* could be captured in one place / a shared test.)

### 4. N+1 warehouse round-trips in the SQL profiler
[`profile_table`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L304) issues 1 aggregate query + one top-values query **per column** in a Python loop. For a 50-column profile that's 51 sequential warehouse queries; against a cold SQL warehouse (~1s/query) that's ~50s of wall time for an interactive authoring aid. Acceptable for v1 (≤50-col cap), but consider batching the top-values queries (UNION ALL / a single windowed query) if profiler latency becomes a problem.

### 5. Parquet footer read twice (minor)
[`_read_dataframe`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L440) reads `pq.ParquetFile(raw).schema.names` to filter requested columns to those present, then re-parses with `pd.read_parquet(columns=present)`. The footer read is cheap, and it's needed so `read_parquet` doesn't raise on an absent column — but the column-presence logic now lives in two places (the pre-read filter and `profile_dataframe`'s missing-column 422 check). Worth a glance to ensure they can't diverge.

Pick up opportunistically; suggest a single cleanup PR (`Fixes #<this>`).
=============== #317 ===============
## Move barStatus() into resultsFormat.ts next to RUN_STATUS_COLORS

Follow-up from the /code-review of PR #315 (live run-progress drawer, A3).

`barStatus()` in [LiveRunProgress.tsx](frontend/src/components/runs/LiveRunProgress.tsx) is a parallel, hand-maintained switch over the `RunStatus` union (run lifecycle → antd `Progress` status). It lives apart from the rest of the run-status presentation in [resultsFormat.ts](frontend/src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts), which exists precisely to be the framework-free, unit-tested home for these status→presentation mappings (`RUN_STATUS_COLORS`, `pipelineStatusColor`, …).

**Action:** move `barStatus` into `resultsFormat.ts` next to `RUN_STATUS_COLORS` so the run-status mappings sit together and are reviewed/added-to as one unit, and give it the unit-test treatment that module documents as its reason to exist. Pure refactor, no behavior change.

Note: a new `RunStatus` value currently falls through `barStatus`'s if-chain to `'normal'` silently — consider a `Record<RunStatus, …>` for exhaustiveness while moving it.

Derive the supported set from `format_from_path`, or drop the redundant check.

### 3. Duplicated stat contract: SQL vs pandas
The per-column statistic definitions (null_fraction = nulls/row_count with the row_count==0 guard, distinct excludes nulls, top_values = non-null desc by count) are implemented twice with no shared code — once in SQL via [`assemble_profile`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L195) and once in pandas via [`profile_dataframe`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L362). A definitional change must be made in both or the two datasource families disagree for the same logical data. (Hard to fully DRY across SQL vs pandas, but the *contract* could be captured in one place / a shared test.)

### 4. N+1 warehouse round-trips in the SQL profiler
[`profile_table`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L304) issues 1 aggregate query + one top-values query **per column** in a Python loop. For a 50-column profile that's 51 sequential warehouse queries; against a cold SQL warehouse (~1s/query) that's ~50s of wall time for an interactive authoring aid. Acceptable for v1 (≤50-col cap), but consider batching the top-values queries (UNION ALL / a single windowed query) if profiler latency becomes a problem.

### 5. Parquet footer read twice (minor)
[`_read_dataframe`](backend/app/services/profile_service.py#L440) reads `pq.ParquetFile(raw).schema.names` to filter requested columns to those present, then re-parses with `pd.read_parquet(columns=present)`. The footer read is cheap, and it's needed so `read_parquet` doesn't raise on an absent column — but the column-presence logic now lives in two places (the pre-read filter and `profile_dataframe`'s missing-column 422 check). Worth a glance to ensure they can't diverge.

Pick up opportunistically; suggest a single cleanup PR (`Fixes #<this>`).
=============== #317 ===============
## Move barStatus() into resultsFormat.ts next to RUN_STATUS_COLORS

Follow-up from the /code-review of PR #315 (live run-progress drawer, A3).

`barStatus()` in [LiveRunProgress.tsx](frontend/src/components/runs/LiveRunProgress.tsx) is a parallel, hand-maintained switch over the `RunStatus` union (run lifecycle → antd `Progress` status). It lives apart from the rest of the run-status presentation in [resultsFormat.ts](frontend/src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts), which exists precisely to be the framework-free, unit-tested home for these status→presentation mappings (`RUN_STATUS_COLORS`, `pipelineStatusColor`, …).

**Action:** move `barStatus` into `resultsFormat.ts` next to `RUN_STATUS_COLORS` so the run-status mappings sit together and are reviewed/added-to as one unit, and give it the unit-test treatment that module documents as its reason to exist. Pure refactor, no behavior change.

Note: a new `RunStatus` value currently falls through `barStatus`'s if-chain to `'normal'` silently — consider a `Record<RunStatus, …>` for exhaustiveness while moving it.
