---

## Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling

**Exit gate:** Async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational.

### Async execution backend (10 tasks — 10/10 ✅)
- [x] ✅ Celery + Redis background task runner for GX scan execution — `run_suite` task + `run_service` — landed early via [PR 4a](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/74) + [PR 4c-i](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/78) + [PR 4c-ii](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/79)
- [x] ✅ Generalise `run_suite` worker dispatch — select the `CheckRunner` by `connection.type` via the runner registry (`build_check_runner`), replacing the Snowflake-hardcoded wiring in `worker/tasks.py`; the seam that makes the flat-file / UC run paths route correctly and post-v1 RDBMS adapters a drop-in — [PR #146-fix](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/146) _(added per [ADR 0011](adr/0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md); profiler dispatch collapsed onto a parallel registry in the same effort, [#147](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/147))_
- [x] ✅ **Check target table/path** — modeled as a **per-suite** datasource-shaped `Suite.target` (JSONB, shaped like the column-profiler request: `table`/`schema`/`catalog`/`path`/`file_format`), since `execute_run` runs all of a suite's checks against one batch (the GX data-asset-per-suite shape). `services/run_target.resolve_target` resolves it per connection type to the runner's `(table, schema, catalog)` triple (flat-file path rides the table-shaped `CheckRunner`'s `table` slot); `validate_target` is the write-time 422 guard on suite create/update. Migration `b1f2c3d4e5a6` (additive nullable column, tested up/down on Postgres). **Dispatch ungated**: `_trigger_suites` now hands each created run to `run_suite.delay` (lazy import — worker↔service cycle), broker-fail marks the run `failed`; `run_suite` resolves the target from the suite (targetless → clean `failed`), so the worker no longer takes a `table` arg; stale `until_week3` wording removed. 13 resolver units + worker/orchestration/suite-API target tests. _(Re-tracked from Week 3; [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215); PR-C0a)_
- [x] ✅ **Manual run trigger + run/result read API** — `POST /suites/{id}/run` queues a `Run` and dispatches it via the shared `run_dispatch.dispatch_run` helper (**edit**-gated — the capability ladder grants "trigger runs" at edit); the suite target (#215) is resolved **up front** so a targetless/misconfigured suite fails fast with 422 instead of a queued→failed run, and a broker outage marks the run `failed` + returns 503 (same contract as the probe). New `api/v1/runs.py` read surface for the results page: `GET /runs?suite_id=&status=&limit=` and `GET /runs/{id}` (run + joined `Result`s) are **suite-scoped** — the list filters to accessible suites and the detail gates on `require_permission(view)`, so per-suite sharing + existence-hiding hold (exactly why direct-Postgres/Grafana is rejected as the primary surface); `GET /pipeline_runs?provider=&status=` is the orchestration monitoring feed (auth-only, not suite-scoped). Read helpers live in `run_service.list_runs/get_run/list_results` + `orchestration_service.list_pipeline_runs`. 14 DB-backed API tests (trigger happy/targetless-422/edit-403/no-access-404/broker-503; list scoping+filters+limit; detail+results / 404s; pipeline filters + auth). _(Run-enablement gap; PR-C0b; gates the Week-6 Results page PR-C1)_
- [x] ✅ Run progress API — poll endpoint returning per-check live status — `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (suite-scoped): run lifecycle status + per-check resolution (status or null=pending) + a status histogram, the compact shape the live-progress UI polls. DB-driven (worker writes status + result rows); GX's atomic batch means per-check rows land at completion, so this reports lifecycle + final resolution (documented). **[PR #301](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/301)** (A1)
- [x] ✅ Cancel run endpoint — gracefully terminate in-progress Celery task — `POST /runs/{id}/cancel` (edit-gated, suite-scoped, 409 if terminal): marks `cancelled` + best-effort `revoke` of the captured `runs.celery_task_id` (migration `d5e6f7a8b9c0`). Cooperative — no SIGKILL mid-GX: worker skips a cancelled run at pickup + `execute_run` won't overwrite a mid-run cancel. Deterministic for queued, best-effort for in-flight. Folded **#227** (one dispatch-failure shape in `run_dispatch`, aligning probe's `finished_at`). **[PR #302](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/302)** (A2)
- [x] ✅ Run history retention policy — configurable purge of results older than N days — **PII-minimisation sweep** (not a row delete: ADR 0012 wants `metric_value` trends to outlive samples). Daily Celery-beat `purge_sample_failures` → `run_service.purge_expired_sample_failures` scrubs `sample_failures` (the only potentially-PII result column) to SQL NULL + stamps `sample_failures_purged_at` on results older than the configurable `sample_failures_retention_days` (default 30). Honoured the pre-built seam (the column + setting + the three "retention sweep" comments existed with no writer). Targets only real sample *objects* via `jsonb_typeof` (the JSONB column stores Python `None` as JSON `'null'`, so `IS NOT NULL` over-matches every passing/errored row); naturally idempotent (scrubbed → SQL NULL → excluded); `retention_days <= 0` disables. 6 DB-backed sweep tests + 1 task-wiring unit test. **(A8)**
- [x] ✅ Flat file run path — resolve batch, load via Pandas, execute GX suite — batch-aware target (regex `pattern` + `latest`/`specific` strategy + `prefix`) resolved live via `run_target.materialize_path` → `flatfile.resolve_batch_file` (Week-3 seam); literal `{path}` unchanged. The pandas load + GX execution already lived in `FlatFileCheckRunner` (Week 3). **[PR #298](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/298)** (A4)
- [x] ✅ UC run path — submit job to Databricks SQL Warehouse, execute GX suite — `UnityCatalogCheckRunner` reads the target table via the Databricks SQL Warehouse into a pandas DataFrame + runs GX in-process (DQX swap-in shape, §5), wired through the runner registry (#146) + per-suite target (#215); shipped Week 3, worker catalog-threading coverage closed in A5. Live SQL-Warehouse read is the deferred smoke. **[PR #299](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/299)** (A5)
- [x] ✅ **Scheduling backend** — cron-driven suite runs make "scheduling operational" (the backend half of the Execution-UI scheduled-runs table). New `schedules` table (migration `f7a8b9c0d1e2`, additive; cascade-deleted with the suite) holding a 5-field `cron` + IANA `timezone` (DST-aware) + precomputed `next_run_at` (indexed) per suite. `services/cron.py` wraps `croniter` + `zoneinfo` (validate cron/tz → 422; **no-backfill** `next_fire` = next fire strictly after the base, so a downtime gap fires once, not per missed slot). `schedule_service` CRUD mirrors `trigger_binding_service` (suite-permission-gated: `edit` to mutate, `view` to read; `next_run_at` recomputed on cadence-change / re-enable). `api/v1/schedules.py` (POST/GET/GET{id}/PATCH/DELETE). New Celery-beat tick `dispatch_due_schedules` (60s) scans `enabled, next_run_at<=now` with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (no double-fire across overlapping ticks), resolves the suite target up front (invalid → skip + advance, never a queued→failed run), queues a `Run` (`triggered_by=schedule:<id>`) + dispatches via the shared `run_dispatch` (broker-fail → `mark_dispatch_failed` #227). `cron.validate_cron` probes a fire so an impossible-calendar cron (Feb 30 — `croniter.is_valid` passes it) is a clean 422, not a 500-on-create / crashed dispatcher tick; the dispatcher self-disables such a row instead of hot-looping. 37 tests (cron units incl. DST spring-forward/fall-back + no-backfill + impossible-date; DB-backed CRUD/authz/422 API; dispatcher due/future/disabled/invalid-target/invalid-cron/no-backfill/broker-fail). **(A7)**

> **Operational result statuses ([#122](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/122) — DONE, closed):** both halves shipped. **`error`** ([PR #297](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/297)) — a check GX can't *evaluate* (per-expectation exception, e.g. a missing column) persists as `error` (no severity, no `metric_value`) instead of being conflated with a data `fail`, never failing its siblings or the run. **`skip`** ([PR #298](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/298), with the flat-file batch run path) — a genuinely-absent batch maps `BatchNotFoundError` → `run_service.skip_run` (every check `skip`, run still succeeds), not a failure. Both values were already in the `RESULT_STATUSES` CHECK (Week-3 schema seam); these emit them. Unit + real-GX + Postgres-integration tests for each.

### ADF + Airflow hybrid monitoring (webhook + polling fallback) (4 tasks — 4/4 ✅)
- [x] ✅ Celery beat task — poll ADF REST API every 10 min for **succeeded** runs (skip pipelines updated by webhook recently) — provider-agnostic `poll_orchestration_runs` beat task through the `OrchestrationProvider` seam — [PR #171](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/189)
- [x] ✅ Celery beat task — poll Airflow REST API `dagRuns` every 10 min — same provider-agnostic beat task (ADF + Airflow share it) — [PR #171](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/189) _(added per ADR 0004; not in roadmap)_
- [x] ✅ Gap recovery logic — on startup + every 30 min, fetch last hour of run statuses — provider-agnostic `recover_orchestration_gaps` beat task reuses the `_poll_orchestration_runs` pipeline with a 1-hour `lookback` (vs the 10-min poll's 15-min window); idempotent upsert + `skip_updated_since` make overlap harmless. New 30-min beat entry + a one-off on worker boot via the `worker_ready` signal (enqueued by name, broker-failure swallowed). **[PR #307](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/307)** (B2)
- [x] ✅ `GET /api/v1/orchestration/pipelines` — latest status per pipeline/DAG, provider-agnostic — one row per distinct `(provider, pipeline_or_dag_id, env)` carrying its most-recent run, via Postgres `DISTINCT ON` (no provider branching); auth-only gated (monitoring, not suite-scoped); `provider`/`env`/`limit` filters. Recency = `COALESCE(started_at, created_at)` so a fresh failure event with no start time isn't masked by an older fully-timed run (`/code-review` catch). **[PR #305](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/305)** (B1); follow-up [#306](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/306) (validate provider/env → 422 on both read endpoints)

> **`trigger_bindings` CRUD** (provider-agnostic suite-run triggers — `provider`/`pipeline_or_dag_id`/`env` → `suite_id`) landed early via [PR #172](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/190), unblocking the trigger-on-success path skeletoned in Week 2 PR 8.

### Execution UI (5 tasks — 4/5 ✅)
- [x] ✅ Run now panel — suite picker, env / datasource, notification target — `RunNowPanel` (`components/runs/`): a **cross-suite** run launcher (modal off a **Run now** button in the Results header), distinct from the suite-detail **Run** button (#245) which runs one suite in context. A searchable suite `Select` filtered to the `owner`/`admin`/`edit` ladder the backend `POST /suites/{id}/run` enforces (view-only suites never appear); a read-only `Descriptions` readout of the picked suite's **env** (tag) + **datasource** (connection name + type) + **target** (one-line `catalog.schema.table` / flat-file `path`, mirroring `suiteTarget.ts`). A targetless suite blocks **Run** with an inline reason rather than a backend 422. On trigger it hands off to the shared `LiveRunProgress` drawer (modal closes; run watched + cancellable). **Notification target ships as a clearly-labelled disabled placeholder** ("coming in Week 6") — no notification backend exists yet (Teams/`ResultPublisher` alert routing is Week 6), so it doesn't jump the gun. Self-contained (owns its data fetch + progress drawer) so the Week-6 UI rework can drop it onto the dedicated Execution page unchanged. 3 component tests (no-runnable empty state, env/datasource/target render + trigger, targetless-suite block).
- [x] ✅ Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button — `LiveRunProgress` drawer (`components/runs/`): opens on the run id returned by the manual **Run** button (#245) instead of bouncing to `/results`, and polls `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (#301) on a self-scheduling timeout (not `setInterval` — no overlapping fetches) until the run is terminal (`succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled`), then stops. Shows the run-status tag + a completed/total `Progress` bar + a per-check list (a `Spin`+"pending" while a check's `status` is null, its severity `Tag` once resolved). An editor (`canRun`) gets a **Cancel** button (`POST /runs/{id}/cancel` #302) on a non-terminal run; cancel reflects the terminal state immediately + the next poll confirms it. On terminal, a "View full results →" link to `/results`. Body keyed by run id so opening a new run restarts polling; `pollMs` is injectable for deterministic tests. 7 component tests (pending/resolved render, poll-until-terminal-then-stop, cancel happy path, cancel hidden when terminal / non-editor, fetch-error alert). **(A3)**
- [x] ✅ Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules — `SchedulesPanel` (`components/suites/`): suite-detail card over the A7 schedules CRUD (#314) — an antd table (cron · timezone · next run · last run · status · delete) + an add form (cron `Input` + IANA-timezone `Select`, default UTC). **Pause/resume** is the `enabled` toggle (`PATCH /schedules/{id}`); **delete** is `DELETE /schedules/{id}`; **create** is `POST /schedules`. Edit-gated (`canManage` = `canRun`): viewers see a read-only `enabled`/`paused` tag, no controls — matching the backend gate. Timezone list comes from `Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone')` (no hardcoded table; UTC-first). Cron/timezone validated server-side → 422 surfaced inline. `api/schedules.ts` + 6 component tests (list, empty, create-with-UTC-default, pause-toggle, delete, viewer-read-only). **(A6)**
- [ ] ⬜ Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results
- [x] ✅ **Suite Triggers panel** — UI over the `trigger_bindings` CRUD (#172): bind/unbind a pipeline/DAG (`provider` + `pipeline_or_dag_id` + `env`) to a suite so it runs on the pipeline's success. **Suite-level**, not a check field (orchestration providers are never a datasource/check attribute, CLAUDE.md §4); inline `TriggersPanel` card on the suite detail — list + enable/disable toggle + remove + add form (ADF/Airflow · pipeline-or-DAG id · env), edit-gated (read-only for viewers). `api/triggerBindings.ts` + 6 component tests. _(PR #247; closes [#216](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/216))_

**Week 5 total: 18 / 19** _(early credit: Celery task runner in PR 4; worker runner-dispatch #146; ADF + Airflow 10-min polling #171; plus `trigger_bindings` CRUD #172 out-of-band; check target-table + dispatch ungate #215 (PR-C0a); manual run trigger + run/result read API (PR-C0b); **Suite Triggers UI #216 (PR #247)**. **This stretch (Phase 1–4):** `error` status #297, flat-file batch + `skip` #298, UC run-path coverage #299, run progress API #301, cancel run + #227 #302, `GET /orchestration/pipelines` #305, gap-recovery beat #307, **scheduling backend (A7)**, **live run-progress UI (A3)**, **scheduled-runs UI (A6)**, **run-history retention (A8)**, **run-now panel**. +3 tasks re-tracked here: check target-table #215 (slipped from W3), Suite Triggers UI #216, and the run-enablement read API (PR-C0b); +1 added: the scheduling backend (A7), the backend the roadmap's scheduled-runs UI task implies. The **manual Run button** (PR #245) + the **run-target editor** (PR #246) also shipped earlier this week. **Remaining (1):** recent-runs audit table)_

---

## Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting

**Exit gate:** Full results dashboard live across all source types; alerts firing with suppression.

### Results dashboard (10 tasks — 2/10 ✅)
> **Build as an in-app React page** (the GX-Cloud-style redesign Phase C), **not Grafana** — reading Postgres directly would bypass DataQ's per-suite sharing + PII redaction; Grafana is deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on ([ADR 0018](adr/0018-results-surface-and-grafana-deferral.md), accepted). The Week-5 run-enablement backend it gates on is **done**: `runs.py` read endpoints (`GET /runs`, `GET /runs/{id}`, `GET /pipeline_runs`) + `POST /suites/{id}/run` (PR-C0b); [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target (PR-C0a). The `results.metric_value`/`duration_ms` columns were seam-built for these trend charts (ADR 0012).
- [x] ✅ **Results page scaffold** (PR-C1) — the in-app `/results` page + sidebar nav (Connections · Suites · **Results** · Profile). **Runs tab**: a runs table (suite name, status, triggered-by, started, duration) with a status filter, each row opening a **run-detail drawer** that drills into the per-check results (check name + expectation, severity tag, `metric_value`, observed value). **Pipeline runs tab**: the orchestration monitoring feed (`pipeline_runs`) with a provider filter. Pure presentation helpers (`resultsFormat.ts` — duration/timestamp formatters, status→colour maps) are unit-tested; `api/runs.ts` mirrors the C0b schemas. Demo seed (`demo_data.py`) now lands runs + results (pass/pass/warn/fail spread) + two pipeline-runs + suite targets so the page has real content. 10 vitest + 2 Playwright (`results.spec.ts`). _The rich widgets below build on this scaffold._
- [ ] ⬜ Health score stat cards + 7-day trend chart
- [ ] ⬜ Per-suite pass / fail progress bars — warn / fail / critical breakdown
- [ ] 🟡 Results filter bar — env, datasource type, suite, date range, status — _run **status** filter + pipeline **provider** filter shipped (PR-C1); env/datasource/suite/date-range pending_
- [ ] 🟡 Failed check drill-down — sample failing rows from GX result — _per-check drill-down shipped (PR-C1); **sample rows withheld** pending row-level PII redaction ([#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226), ADR 0018)_
- [ ] ⬜ Per-check historical trend chart
- [ ] 🟡 Orchestration status panel — pipeline/DAG status, polls every 30s, correlated DQ result — _pipeline-runs feed + status shipped (PR-C1, `/pipeline_runs` tab); 30s auto-poll + DQ-run correlation pending_
- [ ] ⬜ Datasource type filter — Snowflake / flat file / Unity Catalog toggle
- [ ] ⬜ CSV + PDF export of results
- [x] ✅ Severity badge colours — green / amber / red / dark red — severity-tier (pass/warn/fail/critical) + run-status tag colour maps in `resultsFormat.ts`, rendered as antd Tags on the runs table + run detail (PR-C1)
- [ ] ⬜ Health score weighting — apply warn/fail/critical penalty weights

### Consistency & transaction hardening (added — from the ACID/consistency review) (4 items — 1/4)
> Follow-ups surfaced by the ACID / SCD review (2026-06-20). Single-DB ACID is sound (Postgres + unit-of-work + idempotent `ON CONFLICT` upserts); these close the deliberate gaps. Not in the original 100-task roadmap — tracked here so they aren't lost.
- [ ] ⬜ **Trigger-dedup race** — `_trigger_suites` is a non-atomic SELECT-then-INSERT with no unique constraint on `runs(suite_id, triggered_by)`; concurrent ingestion (webhook / poll / gap-recovery) can double-trigger a suite run. Fix = partial unique index + `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`. [#308](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/308)
- [ ] ⬜ **Stuck-run reaper** — a run committed `queued` before `send_task` is orphaned forever if the process dies in that window (no reaper; gap-recovery covers `pipeline_runs`, not `runs`). Beat janitor to fail/re-dispatch runs stuck past a threshold. [#309](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/309)
- [x] ✅ **History/audit strategy (ADR)** — decided in [ADR 0020](adr/0020-history-and-audit-strategy.md): per-entity **Type-4** snapshot tables where config history is needed (no SCD-2; credentials never snapshotted; cascade-delete accepted; soft-delete + cross-entity audit log deferred). **First implementation: `connection_versions`** (connection name/config history, snapshot on create + name/config update; secret-only rotation records nothing). [#310](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/310) _(audit-log + soft-delete remain deferred there)_
- [ ] ⬜ **Minor robustness (no separate issue)** — concurrent check-edit collision → 409 not an unhandled 500 (`IntegrityError` on the `uq_check_versions_check_version` backstop); explicit `rollback` in `get_db`'s `except`; document the "read-modify-write needs a unique constraint or `FOR UPDATE`" convention in CONTRIBUTING (no row-locking exists today, so every check-then-write is only as safe as its constraint).

> Closes out the prototype after the Week-6 structural pass: the two screens that need backend (**Admin**, **Settings**) plus the final visual-fidelity pass to the design-system guidelines. Admin needs a real **workspace-admin** authz concept (today `admin` is per-suite only) — the prototype's client-side "View as" switch is **not** used (real authz is server-side, CLAUDE.md §11). **To open the prototype, see the *Design source* note in the Week-6 prototype-adoption group** (project `317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d` via the `DesignSync` tool).

Deliberately unscoped (bypasses owned-or-shared). `/me` exposes `is_workspace_admin`.

Suites / Users / Access tabs on the new endpoint, with a **real 403** `Forbidden` page for non-admins (server-driven via `/me`'s `is_workspace_admin` — *not* a client "View as" toggle), admin-only nav, shared `MeProvider`/`useMe`.

file.content:
- [ ] ⬜ Auth endpoints — login redirect, token refresh, unauthorised → 401
- [x] ✅ **Probe endpoints** (out-of-roadmap) — POST creates+dispatches, idempotent seed, GET results, 404 — against real Postgres — [PR 4c-ii](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/79)
- [x] 🟡 Connection endpoints — CRUD happy paths + validation errors — Snowflake covered (13 TestClient tests: CRUD, 422/404/502, secret-never-leaks, auth gate) — [PR 5](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/85); ADF covered (4 TestClient tests: create, orchestrator 409, second-env 201, type filter) — PR 6; ADLS/S3/UC types follow their CRUD PRs
- [ ] ⬜ Suite & check endpoints — CRUD, share, export / import, dry-run
- [ ] ⬜ Execution endpoints — trigger run, poll progress, cancel, list history
- [ ] ⬜ Results endpoints — dashboard data, drill-down, filters, download
- [x] 🟡 ADF webhook endpoint — valid payload → 200 (recorded/ignored), missing+wrong token → 401, malformed/non-JSON → 422, secret-unconfigured → 503 (9 TestClient tests) — PR 7; duplicate-runId idempotency asserted at the service layer

### Frontend unit tests (Vitest + RTL) (8 tasks — 1/8 ✅)
- [x] ✅ **AuthGate** — 4 tests (dev_bypass renders children, unconfigured banner, real+unauth sign-in button, real+auth renders children) — [PR 3c](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/63)
- [x] 🟡 **API client interceptor** — 3 tests (no-token in dev, Bearer in real-with-account, no-token in real-without-account) — [PR 3c](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/63)
- [ ] ⬜ Login screen — Azure AD button renders, redirects on click
- [ ] ⬜ Connection manager — card per type, status badge colours, re-auth flow
- [ ] ⬜ Check editor — form fields per check type, profiler loads, dry-run, severity tier toggle
- [ ] ⬜ Suite sharing panel — add/remove user, role assignment
- [ ] ⬜ Execution page — run now, progress bar updates, cancel button
- [ ] ⬜ Results dashboard — stat cards, filters, ADF panel, severity badges

### Test infrastructure (3 tasks — 0.5/3 🟡 early)
- [ ] 🟡 Pytest fixtures — transactional Postgres `db_session` fixture + CI postgres service + fake `CheckRunner`/session landed ([PR 4b.1](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/77) + [PR 4c-ii](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/79)); mock GX context + mock webhooks pending
- [ ] ⬜ CI gate — PRs blocked if coverage drops below 80% _(coverage currently ~91%; `--cov-fail-under` still 0 until W8)_
- [ ] 🟡 Test data fixtures — sample suites, check results, run histories — `backend/scripts/demo_data.py` seeds a representative dataset (all six connection types + 3 suites with varied checks + a cross-user share) through the real service layer, wired into `seed_dev`; **run histories still pending** (need the W5 execution path). Paired with `backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py` (full-stack API E2E — see below)

**Week 8 total: ~4 / 26 (early credit from per-slice tests in W1 — overall coverage ~91% backend; frontend ~80% lines)**

> **Full-stack E2E ([#128](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/128) — both halves landed):**
> - **API half** — `backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py` drives the **real** running stack (docker compose + dev-bypass + the demo seed) with no mocks: asserts the six connection types list (secrets never returned), suites + checks read back, severity thresholds round-trip, the create→add-check→dry-run→delete authoring loop, and dry-run failing soft (502/422, not a crash). **12/12 pass.**
> - **Browser half** — `frontend/e2e/` (Playwright) drives the **React UI** the same way a user does (`browser → Vite proxy → api → Postgres`): app-shell loads under dev-bypass, sider nav, seeded connections grouped by type + "Test all" health path, seeded suite → its checks, and a full create-suite → add → delete authoring round-trip. **6/6 pass.** Wired into CI as the `frontend-e2e` job (Postgres + Redis services, migrate + seed, uvicorn on :8000, Playwright starts its own Vite server). See [frontend/e2e/README.md](../frontend/e2e/README.md).
>
> Live datasource `test()`/runs remain the deferred smoke (no real creds — connectivity fails-soft).

> Post-v1 items (marketing landing page, dark mode) are tracked in their own section below and are **excluded** from this weekly aggregate.

> 177 > 100 because ADR 0004 added Airflow tasks, ADR 0011 added two seam tasks (generic runner dispatch, `ResultPublisher`), ADR 0012 added three Week-3 monitor-kind / metric seam tasks, the W5 run-enablement gaps surfaced in review (check target-table #215, Suite Triggers UI #216), the GX-Cloud-style UI redesign added four UI-shape tasks (dedicated/classified connection + check pages), the **DataQ Design System prototype adoption** added eleven tasks (six Week-6 dedicated-page-flow + Dashboard-landing tasks, five Week-7 backend-gated screens + polish), plus PR-review follow-ups not in the original roadmap. Tracked here for honesty.

---

## Post-v1 — deferred prototype items

From the **DataQ Design System** prototype, but out of v1 scope. Not counted in the weekly aggregate. **To open the prototype, see the *Design source* note in the Week-6 prototype-adoption group** (project `317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d` via the `DesignSync` tool).

- [ ] ⬜ **Marketing landing page** (`templates/marketing/`) — sticky nav, hero (live product preview), features bento, social-proof strip, dark CTA closer, footer. **Build when DataQ goes multi-tenant** — single-tenant v1 is an Azure-AD-gated console with no public/unauthenticated surface, so a marketing site has no home yet. Reproduce with real antd (or a static marketing stack) at that point. _Design ref: `templates/marketing/{MarketingNav,Hero,Features,MarketingClose}.jsx` + `templates/marketing/index.html`; readme §"Templates → `templates/marketing/`"._
- [ ] ⬜ **Dark mode** — ship the `[data-theme="dark"]` semantic remap (deep-slate surfaces, lifted status hues for AA contrast) + an opt-in toggle (optional `prefers-color-scheme` auto-switch). **Architecturally ready** — v1 components already reference semantic tokens, so the flip is "zero component changes" *iff* the Week-7 visual-fidelity pass keeps that discipline. v1 ships light-only by decision. _Design ref: `tokens/dark.css` (the `[data-theme="dark"]` scope) + `guidelines/dark-theme.card.html`; readme §"Dark mode (planned post-v1)"._

=== EPIC #174 (Week 5) ===
STATE: OPEN

Tracking epic for **Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling**. Mirrors the roadmap; the task-level source of truth remains [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md). Milestone: `Week 5 — Execution engine + scheduling`.

**Progress (early-credit):** 4 done / 17.

**Async execution backend**
- [x] Celery + Redis background task runner for GX scan execution — landed early (PR 4a/4c)
- [x] Generalise `run_suite` worker dispatch — select `CheckRunner` by `connection.type` via the runner registry (`build_check_runner`) — #146 _(ADR 0011; profiler dispatch collapsed onto a parallel registry, #147)_
- [ ] **Check target table/path** — checks carry no target today (dry-run takes `table` in the body), so the trigger-on-success path is gated (`suite_dispatch_deferred`). The prerequisite for every run path; model the target, migrate, ungate dispatch. Slipped from Week 3 → tracked here — #215
- [x] Run progress API — poll endpoint returning per-check live status — #301 (A1)
- [x] Cancel run endpoint — gracefully terminate in-progress Celery task — #302 (A2; folds #227)
- [x] Run history retention policy — configurable purge of results older than N days — PII-minimisation sweep (keeps the row + `metric_value` so trends survive, ADR 0012): daily `purge_sample_failures` beat → `run_service.purge_expired_sample_failures` scrubs old `sample_failures` to SQL NULL + stamps `sample_failures_purged_at` on results past the configurable `sample_failures_retention_days` (default 30); `jsonb_typeof`-targeted, idempotent (A8)
- [x] Flat file run path — resolve batch, load via Pandas, execute GX suite — #298 (A4)
- [x] UC run path — submit job to Databricks SQL Warehouse, execute GX suite — #299 (A5)
- [x] Scheduling backend — cron-driven suite runs: `schedules` table (cron + IANA tz + indexed `next_run_at`) + `dispatch_due_schedules` 60s beat (no-backfill, DST-aware, `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`) + CRUD API — backend for the scheduled-runs UI — #314 (A7)

**ADF + Airflow hybrid monitoring (webhook + polling fallback)**
- [x] Celery beat task — poll ADF REST API every 10 min for **succeeded** runs, skip recently-webhooked pipelines — provider-agnostic `poll_orchestration_runs` — #171
- [x] Celery beat task — poll Airflow REST API `dagRuns` every 10 min — same provider-agnostic beat task — #171
- [x] Gap recovery logic — on startup + every 30 min, fetch last hour of run statuses and upsert missed events (#307)
- [x] `GET /api/v1/orchestration/pipelines` — latest status per pipeline/DAG, provider-agnostic (#305)

**Execution UI**
- [x] Run now panel — suite picker, env / datasource, notification target
- [x] Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button — `LiveRunProgress` drawer (A3)
- [x] Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules — `SchedulesPanel` over the A7 CRUD (A6)
- [ ] Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results
- [ ] **Suite Triggers panel** — UI over the shipped `trigger_bindings` CRUD (#172): suite-level bind of pipeline/DAG → suite so it runs on the pipeline's success (not a check field, CLAUDE.md §4) — #216

_Out-of-band: `trigger_bindings` CRUD (provider-agnostic suite-run triggers) landed via #172._






=== EPIC #175 (Week 6) ===
STATE: OPEN

Tracking epic for **Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting**. Mirrors the roadmap; the task-level source of truth remains [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md). Milestone: `Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting`.


**Results dashboard**
- [ ] Health score stat cards + 7-day trend chart
- [ ] Per-suite pass / fail progress bars — updated to show warn / fail / critical breakdown
- [ ] Results filter bar — by env, datasource type, suite, date range, status (including warn / fail / critical)
- [ ] Failed check drill-down — sample failing rows from GX result
- [ ] Per-check historical trend chart (not just overall health score)
- [ ] ADF pipeline status panel — polls `GET /api/v1/adf/pipelines` every 30s; status reflects webhook-pushed failures (near real-time) and polling-filled succeeded runs; shows pipeline name, last run time, duration, status badge, and correlated DQ result
- [ ] Datasource type filter — toggle Snowflake / flat file / Unity Catalog on results view
- [ ] CSV + PDF export of results
- [ ] Severity badge colours — green (pass), amber (warn), red (fail), dark red (critical); applied consistently across all result tables, drill-downs, and suite cards
- [ ] Health score weighting — apply agreed penalty weights (warn / fail / critical) in health score calculation; show severity breakdown in the 7-day trend tooltip

**Alerting**
- [ ] Notification config UI — Teams webhook per suite, alert on fail / warn / always
- [ ] Alert suppression / snooze — silence a specific check for N hours
- [ ] Alert dedup — fire on first failure only, not on every subsequent scheduled run
- [ ] Teams adaptive card payload — check name, datasource, table / file, observed vs expected
- [ ] Severity-aware alert routing — `warn` fires a quieter notification (different card colour, no @mention); `fail` fires standard alert; `critical` fires with @channel mention and distinct card styling


**Prototype adoption — dedicated-page flows + Dashboard landing (added — not in original roadmap)**
> DataQ Design System prototype adoption; mirrors the matching group in [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) (the task-level source of truth).
- [ ] Nav / shell restructure — Dashboard landing entry + reordered sider + footer group (Settings · Admin · Documentation); login redirect `/` → `/dashboard`
- [ ] Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard page (`/dashboard`, `DashboardScreen`) — hosts the Week-6 dashboard widgets; prereq: a chart-library decision
- [ ] New Suite → dedicated page (`/suites/new`, `SuiteFlow`)
- [ ] Edit flows → dedicated pages (connection / suite / check `:id` routes; removes the edit drawers)
- [ ] Results drill-down → dedicated route (`/results/:runId`, `ResultsScreen` detail)
- [ ] Standard error pages (4xx / 5xx) — `ErrorState` in the app shell + router catch-all

content.text: Let me read the Week 4 section I skipped (162–195) to get its subtotals for the reconciliation.

content.content:
162	### Connection manager UI (6 tasks — 6/6 ✅)
163	- [x] ✅ Connection cards — Snowflake (3 envs), ADF, ADLS/S3, Databricks sections with status badges — grouped-by-type cards with env tag + credential badge + Test action — [PR #191](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/191)
164	- [x] ✅ Add connection drawer — type-specific form fields per connection type — spec-driven `ConnectionTypeFields` from `CONNECTION_FORM_SPECS` (all six types; Snowflake password/key-pair auth toggle) — [PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196) (+ Snowflake key-pair backend [PR #193](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/193))
165	- [x] ✅ Connection health page — bulk test, live status, re-auth surface — "Test all" header action tests every connection concurrently; each card shows a live health badge (testing / healthy / unreachable) and surfaces an inline **Re-authenticate** link on failure — [PR #208](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/208) _(folded into the connections list rather than a duplicate route)_
166	- [x] ✅ Connection re-auth UI — surface expired tokens, inline refresh action — `ReauthModal` (single-secret rotate+verify) + edit drawer + delete via the card actions menu — [PR #198](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/198)
167	- [x] ✅ ADLS/S3 connection form — account URL, SAS toggle — covered by the spec-driven drawer ([PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196)) _(container browser + managed-identity/IAM-role modes deferred with the backend, ADR 0010/0011)_
168	- [x] ✅ Databricks connection form — workspace URL, PAT, warehouse id — covered by the spec-driven drawer ([PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196)) _(live SQL-Warehouse picker deferred; warehouse id is a text field)_
169	
170	### Check editor UI (9 tasks — 9/9 ✅)
171	- [x] ✅ Suite list + detail two-panel layout, environment badge on each suite — selectable suites list ←→ detail (connection chip + env tag + checks) — [PR #200](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/200)
172	- [x] ✅ Form-based check editor — catalog-driven expectation picker + dynamic typed config fields + create/edit/delete — `expectationCatalog.ts` + `CheckDrawer` ([PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)). GX expectations are datasource-agnostic in v1, so one catalog serves all four types
173	- [x] ✅ Flat file check editor — file path + format selector — the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** on the suite drawer ([PR #246](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/246)) authors a flat-file target (`path` + optional CSV/Parquet `file_format`), so ADLS/S3 suites become runnable; the generic catalog editor ([PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)) already covers flat-file expectations. _(Container/regex **batch-resolution** inputs remain a post-v1 polish — needs the flat-file listing surface.)_
174	- [x] ✅ Unity Catalog check editor — catalog / schema / table inputs — the run-target editor ([PR #246](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/246)) authors a UC target (`catalog` + `table` + optional `schema`), making UC suites runnable; expectations covered by the generic catalog editor. _(A **cascading enumerated** 3-level picker is optional polish — blocked on a Databricks catalog-listing API.)_
175	- [x] ✅ **Monaco SQL editor for custom check** — a "Custom SQL" category authoring a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation` (ADR 0019): a lazy-loaded, locally-bundled Monaco editor (`SqlEditorField`, its own `monaco` Vite chunk so the eager bundle is untouched) + a client-side mirror of the backend read-only/single-statement guardrail (`customSql.ts`) for inline feedback. Gated to SQL datasources (Snowflake / Unity Catalog) in both the create page and the edit drawer via `expectationsByCategoryFor(connectionType)`. _(backend unblocked by ADR 0019 / [PR #258](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/258); Snowflake keyword autocomplete deferred — Monaco ships SQL syntax highlighting only)_
176	- [x] ✅ Column profiler panel — inline in check editor, loads on table / file selection — `ColumnProfilePanel` (shared by the create page `CheckNew` + the edit drawer `CheckDrawer`): a collapsed-by-default panel that profiles one column of the suite's run target (#215) via `POST /suites/{id}/profile` (no persistence) and renders null count/fraction, distinct count, min/max, and top values — so the author can ground a range / allowed-set / null threshold in real data before saving. The profiled column **pre-fills from the check's `column` config field** (render-phase prop-sync, like `DryRunPreview`) but stays editable to explore neighbouring columns; `table`/`schema`/`catalog` (SQL) or `path`/`file_format` (flat file) are pulled from the suite target. Disabled with a reason until the suite has a table/file target + a column is entered; backend limits (no credential / unreachable / unsupported type) surface as a clean error alert. 4 vitest (disabled-states / pre-filled-profile with request-shape assertion / error). _(PR-D2; `ColumnProfilePanel.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `profileColumns`)_
177	- [x] ✅ **Check dry-run button** — preview pass/fail inline before saving — `DryRunPreview` component (shared by the create page `CheckNew` + the edit drawer `CheckDrawer`): runs the in-progress check against the suite's live target via `POST /suites/{id}/checks/dryrun` and renders the severity outcome (status tag + `metric_value` + observed/expected), **no persistence**. Reuses `buildCheckPayload` (so the preview runs exactly the saved check's config/thresholds) + the severity colour map; `table`/`schema` come from the suite's run target (#215, fetched via new `getSuite`). Disabled with a reason until an expectation is picked + the suite has a table target; backend limits (Snowflake-only, no-credential/unreachable) surface as a clean error alert (dev fail-softs to a 502 with fake creds). 4 vitest (disabled-states / success-outcome with request-shape assertion / error) + an e2e button-enabled assertion. _(PR-D1; `DryRunPreview.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `getSuite`/`dryRunCheck`)_
178	- [x] ✅ **Check version history drawer — see previous config before overwriting** — a `check_versions` table snapshots a check's editable state (name/expectation/config/thresholds + author) on create, on every *real* update (no-op PATCHes skipped via `session.is_modified`), and per imported check; `GET /suites/{id}/checks/{cid}/versions` (view-gated, newest-first, author resolved server-side). The **History** button on `CheckDrawer` opens `CheckHistoryDrawer` — newest-first list, latest tagged **Current**, each row showing expectation label + pretty-printed config + `Warn ≥/Fail ≥/Critical ≥` thresholds + author/timestamp. View-only; **restore deferred to [#283](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/283)**. Backend migration verified up/down; 7 backend + 6 frontend tests. _(#280; backend [PR #281](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/281) + frontend [PR #282](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/282); ADR 0019 custom-SQL configs stay readable via pretty-print)_
179	- [x] ✅ Severity tier toggle in check editor — three-threshold UI — optional warn/fail/critical inputs banding the unexpected-% (ADR 0016), blank = binary — [PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)
180	
181	### Access & admin UI (3 tasks — 3/3 ✅)
182	- [x] ✅ Suite sharing panel — add / remove users, assign roles inline — **Share** button on the suite detail opens `SharePanel` (drawer): lists collaborators by name (from the enriched `ShareRead`) and, for owner/admin, adds/changes/removes them. Add picker is a debounced `GET /users/search` type-ahead filtering out the owner + already-shared users; permission is the view/edit/admin ladder. Gated on **`SuiteRead.my_permission`** — a new field stamping the caller's effective level (owner/admin/edit/view), populated on create/get/update + batched across the list via `suite_authz.effective_permissions` (one shares `IN`-query, no N+1) — so a non-owner admin gets the manage controls and a viewer a read-only list. Backend enablers landed first: **PR-E2** (`GET /users/search` behind a FastAPI-free `user_service` — case-insensitive email/display_name substring, 2-char min, limit≤50, LIKE-escaped — + `ShareRead` enriched with `email`/`display_name` via a joined read-only `Share.user`). 6 component tests (list-by-name, empty, search→grant with owner/existing filtered, change, revoke, read-only non-admin) + backend `my_permission` + `/users/search` tests. _(PR-E2 + PR-E3)_
183	- [x] ✅ Admin page — list all suites, all users, access overview — **workspace-admin control centre** shipped (backend + frontend, #289, closes the long-standing backend block). New **workspace-admin** authz primitive (config `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist, case-insensitive on `user.email` — generic identity axis, no Azure/Entra claim read; **no migration**) distinct from the per-suite `view < edit < admin < owner` ladder. Backend: `admin_service` (`list_all_suites` / `list_all_users` / `list_all_access`, deliberately **unscoped** — bypasses the owned-or-shared scoping `list_suites` applies) + `GET /admin/{suites,users,access}` gated by `require_workspace_admin` (real **403**); `/me` now exposes `is_workspace_admin`. Frontend: `/admin` page (Suites · Users · Access tabs) + `Forbidden` 403 page + admin-only nav item, gated on `/me`'s server-driven flag via a shared `MeProvider`/`useMe` (one `/me` fetch, reused by Profile; resets on sign-out). 23 tests (backend: scoping-bypass, counts, case-insensitive, non-admin 403, `/me` flag; frontend: forbidden gate, all three tabs, load error, MeProvider sign-out reset). _(This pulled the Week-7 prototype-adoption tasks "workspace-admin list-all endpoint" + "Admin control centre page" forward.)_
184	- [x] ✅ Suite export / import UI (download JSON, upload JSON) — **Export** button on the suite detail header downloads `<suite>.json` from `GET /suites/{id}/export` via a transient-anchor blob (`utils/download.ts` — `downloadJson` + `toFilenameStem`); **Import** button on the suites header opens `ImportSuiteDrawer` — drag/drop a `.json`, client-side parse + shape-check (fail-fast on non-suite/malformed JSON), pick a target connection, `POST /suites/import`. The parsed document is handed back **untouched** so thresholds round-trip exactly (string-or-number Decimal encoding never coerced). 11 tests (download lib: stem slug + blob/anchor/revoke; drawer: valid→import-with-faithful-document, non-suite/malformed-JSON rejection, connection-gated Import). _(PR-E1; backend ready from Week 3)_
185	
186	### GX-Cloud-style UI redesign (added — not in original roadmap) (4 tasks — 4/4 ✅)
187	> Model the UI on [GX Cloud](https://greatexpectations.io/gx-cloud/): dedicated **full-page create flows** that **classify** what you're creating, keeping the lighter drawers for *edit*. Plan: `~/.claude/plans/fancy-moseying-raccoon.md`. Decided: in-app results page (Grafana deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on, never the per-suite authz path — ADR 0018 pending); full-page create + drawer edit; connections/checks redesign now (backends ready), results after the Week-5 run-enablement backend.
188	- [x] ✅ **Connections — dedicated, classified add page** — `/connections/new` splits the type picker into **Data sources** vs **Orchestration** sections (CLAUDE.md §4), then the spec-driven form; the list page is grouped the same way. `CONNECTION_KIND` is the single source for the split. Edit/re-auth/delete stay on the drawer. _(PR-A1; `ConnectionNew.tsx` + sectioned `Connections.tsx`)_

The natural home for the [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target table/path field. _(PR-B2; `CheckNew.tsx`)_

file.content:
### Connection manager UI (6 tasks — 6/6 ✅)
- [x] ✅ Connection cards — Snowflake (3 envs), ADF, ADLS/S3, Databricks sections with status badges — grouped-by-type cards with env tag + credential badge + Test action — [PR #191](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/191)
- [x] ✅ Add connection drawer — type-specific form fields per connection type — spec-driven `ConnectionTypeFields` from `CONNECTION_FORM_SPECS` (all six types; Snowflake password/key-pair auth toggle) — [PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196) (+ Snowflake key-pair backend [PR #193](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/193))
- [x] ✅ Connection health page — bulk test, live status, re-auth surface — "Test all" header action tests every connection concurrently; each card shows a live health badge (testing / healthy / unreachable) and surfaces an inline **Re-authenticate** link on failure — [PR #208](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/208) _(folded into the connections list rather than a duplicate route)_
- [x] ✅ Connection re-auth UI — surface expired tokens, inline refresh action — `ReauthModal` (single-secret rotate+verify) + edit drawer + delete via the card actions menu — [PR #198](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/198)
- [x] ✅ ADLS/S3 connection form — account URL, SAS toggle — covered by the spec-driven drawer ([PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196)) _(container browser + managed-identity/IAM-role modes deferred with the backend, ADR 0010/0011)_
- [x] ✅ Databricks connection form — workspace URL, PAT, warehouse id — covered by the spec-driven drawer ([PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196)) _(live SQL-Warehouse picker deferred; warehouse id is a text field)_

### Check editor UI (9 tasks — 9/9 ✅)
- [x] ✅ Suite list + detail two-panel layout, environment badge on each suite — selectable suites list ←→ detail (connection chip + env tag + checks) — [PR #200](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/200)
- [x] ✅ Form-based check editor — catalog-driven expectation picker + dynamic typed config fields + create/edit/delete — `expectationCatalog.ts` + `CheckDrawer` ([PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)). GX expectations are datasource-agnostic in v1, so one catalog serves all four types
- [x] ✅ Flat file check editor — file path + format selector — the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** on the suite drawer ([PR #246](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/246)) authors a flat-file target (`path` + optional CSV/Parquet `file_format`), so ADLS/S3 suites become runnable; the generic catalog editor ([PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)) already covers flat-file expectations. _(Container/regex **batch-resolution** inputs remain a post-v1 polish — needs the flat-file listing surface.)_
- [x] ✅ Unity Catalog check editor — catalog / schema / table inputs — the run-target editor ([PR #246](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/246)) authors a UC target (`catalog` + `table` + optional `schema`), making UC suites runnable; expectations covered by the generic catalog editor. _(A **cascading enumerated** 3-level picker is optional polish — blocked on a Databricks catalog-listing API.)_
- [x] ✅ **Monaco SQL editor for custom check** — a "Custom SQL" category authoring a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation` (ADR 0019): a lazy-loaded, locally-bundled Monaco editor (`SqlEditorField`, its own `monaco` Vite chunk so the eager bundle is untouched) + a client-side mirror of the backend read-only/single-statement guardrail (`customSql.ts`) for inline feedback. Gated to SQL datasources (Snowflake / Unity Catalog) in both the create page and the edit drawer via `expectationsByCategoryFor(connectionType)`. _(backend unblocked by ADR 0019 / [PR #258](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/258); Snowflake keyword autocomplete deferred — Monaco ships SQL syntax highlighting only)_
- [x] ✅ Column profiler panel — inline in check editor, loads on table / file selection — `ColumnProfilePanel` (shared by the create page `CheckNew` + the edit drawer `CheckDrawer`): a collapsed-by-default panel that profiles one column of the suite's run target (#215) via `POST /suites/{id}/profile` (no persistence) and renders null count/fraction, distinct count, min/max, and top values — so the author can ground a range / allowed-set / null threshold in real data before saving. The profiled column **pre-fills from the check's `column` config field** (render-phase prop-sync, like `DryRunPreview`) but stays editable to explore neighbouring columns; `table`/`schema`/`catalog` (SQL) or `path`/`file_format` (flat file) are pulled from the suite target. Disabled with a reason until the suite has a table/file target + a column is entered; backend limits (no credential / unreachable / unsupported type) surface as a clean error alert. 4 vitest (disabled-states / pre-filled-profile with request-shape assertion / error). _(PR-D2; `ColumnProfilePanel.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `profileColumns`)_
- [x] ✅ **Check dry-run button** — preview pass/fail inline before saving — `DryRunPreview` component (shared by the create page `CheckNew` + the edit drawer `CheckDrawer`): runs the in-progress check against the suite's live target via `POST /suites/{id}/checks/dryrun` and renders the severity outcome (status tag + `metric_value` + observed/expected), **no persistence**. Reuses `buildCheckPayload` (so the preview runs exactly the saved check's config/thresholds) + the severity colour map; `table`/`schema` come from the suite's run target (#215, fetched via new `getSuite`). Disabled with a reason until an expectation is picked + the suite has a table target; backend limits (Snowflake-only, no-credential/unreachable) surface as a clean error alert (dev fail-softs to a 502 with fake creds). 4 vitest (disabled-states / success-outcome with request-shape assertion / error) + an e2e button-enabled assertion. _(PR-D1; `DryRunPreview.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `getSuite`/`dryRunCheck`)_
- [x] ✅ **Check version history drawer — see previous config before overwriting** — a `check_versions` table snapshots a check's editable state (name/expectation/config/thresholds + author) on create, on every *real* update (no-op PATCHes skipped via `session.is_modified`), and per imported check; `GET /suites/{id}/checks/{cid}/versions` (view-gated, newest-first, author resolved server-side). The **History** button on `CheckDrawer` opens `CheckHistoryDrawer` — newest-first list, latest tagged **Current**, each row showing expectation label + pretty-printed config + `Warn ≥/Fail ≥/Critical ≥` thresholds + author/timestamp. View-only; **restore deferred to [#283](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/283)**. Backend migration verified up/down; 7 backend + 6 frontend tests. _(#280; backend [PR #281](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/281) + frontend [PR #282](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/282); ADR 0019 custom-SQL configs stay readable via pretty-print)_
- [x] ✅ Severity tier toggle in check editor — three-threshold UI — optional warn/fail/critical inputs banding the unexpected-% (ADR 0016), blank = binary — [PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)

### Access & admin UI (3 tasks — 3/3 ✅)
- [x] ✅ Suite sharing panel — add / remove users, assign roles inline — **Share** button on the suite detail opens `SharePanel` (drawer): lists collaborators by name (from the enriched `ShareRead`) and, for owner/admin, adds/changes/removes them. Add picker is a debounced `GET /users/search` type-ahead filtering out the owner + already-shared users; permission is the view/edit/admin ladder. Gated on **`SuiteRead.my_permission`** — a new field stamping the caller's effective level (owner/admin/edit/view), populated on create/get/update + batched across the list via `suite_authz.effective_permissions` (one shares `IN`-query, no N+1) — so a non-owner admin gets the manage controls and a viewer a read-only list. Backend enablers landed first: **PR-E2** (`GET /users/search` behind a FastAPI-free `user_service` — case-insensitive email/display_name substring, 2-char min, limit≤50, LIKE-escaped — + `ShareRead` enriched with `email`/`display_name` via a joined read-only `Share.user`). 6 component tests (list-by-name, empty, search→grant with owner/existing filtered, change, revoke, read-only non-admin) + backend `my_permission` + `/users/search` tests. _(PR-E2 + PR-E3)_
- [x] ✅ Admin page — list all suites, all users, access overview — **workspace-admin control centre** shipped (backend + frontend, #289, closes the long-standing backend block). New **workspace-admin** authz primitive (config `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist, case-insensitive on `user.email` — generic identity axis, no Azure/Entra claim read; **no migration**) distinct from the per-suite `view < edit < admin < owner` ladder. Backend: `admin_service` (`list_all_suites` / `list_all_users` / `list_all_access`, deliberately **unscoped** — bypasses the owned-or-shared scoping `list_suites` applies) + `GET /admin/{suites,users,access}` gated by `require_workspace_admin` (real **403**); `/me` now exposes `is_workspace_admin`. Frontend: `/admin` page (Suites · Users · Access tabs) + `Forbidden` 403 page + admin-only nav item, gated on `/me`'s server-driven flag via a shared `MeProvider`/`useMe` (one `/me` fetch, reused by Profile; resets on sign-out). 23 tests (backend: scoping-bypass, counts, case-insensitive, non-admin 403, `/me` flag; frontend: forbidden gate, all three tabs, load error, MeProvider sign-out reset). _(This pulled the Week-7 prototype-adoption tasks "workspace-admin list-all endpoint" + "Admin control centre page" forward.)_
- [x] ✅ Suite export / import UI (download JSON, upload JSON) — **Export** button on the suite detail header downloads `<suite>.json` from `GET /suites/{id}/export` via a transient-anchor blob (`utils/download.ts` — `downloadJson` + `toFilenameStem`); **Import** button on the suites header opens `ImportSuiteDrawer` — drag/drop a `.json`, client-side parse + shape-check (fail-fast on non-suite/malformed JSON), pick a target connection, `POST /suites/import`. The parsed document is handed back **untouched** so thresholds round-trip exactly (string-or-number Decimal encoding never coerced). 11 tests (download lib: stem slug + blob/anchor/revoke; drawer: valid→import-with-faithful-document, non-suite/malformed-JSON rejection, connection-gated Import). _(PR-E1; backend ready from Week 3)_

### GX-Cloud-style UI redesign (added — not in original roadmap) (4 tasks — 4/4 ✅)
> Model the UI on [GX Cloud](https://greatexpectations.io/gx-cloud/): dedicated **full-page create flows** that **classify** what you're creating, keeping the lighter drawers for *edit*. Plan: `~/.claude/plans/fancy-moseying-raccoon.md`. Decided: in-app results page (Grafana deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on, never the per-suite authz path — ADR 0018 pending); full-page create + drawer edit; connections/checks redesign now (backends ready), results after the Week-5 run-enablement backend.
- [x] ✅ **Connections — dedicated, classified add page** — `/connections/new` splits the type picker into **Data sources** vs **Orchestration** sections (CLAUDE.md §4), then the spec-driven form; the list page is grouped the same way. `CONNECTION_KIND` is the single source for the split. Edit/re-auth/delete stay on the drawer. _(PR-A1; `ConnectionNew.tsx` + sectioned `Connections.tsx`)_
- [x] ✅ **Connections — `ConnectionDrawer` trimmed to edit-only** — create now lives on the page; the drawer drops the type/env Selects + secret + create branch, shows type/env read-only, and the list owns a simpler `editing: Connection | null` state. _(PR-A2)_
- [x] ✅ **Checks — suite selection as a route + categorized expectation picker** — `/suites/:suiteId` makes the selected suite deep-linkable and survives the check-editor round-trip; the catalog gains a `category` (Column values / Table shape) and the picker groups by it (antd optgroups). Reserved Freshness/Volume/Schema-drift categories (ADR 0012) land with the dedicated page. _(PR-B1)_
- [x] ✅ **Checks — dedicated `/suites/:suiteId/checks/new` page** — category → expectation → config/thresholds, with reserved monitor-kind categories (Freshness/Volume/Schema-drift, ADR 0012) shown disabled. Shared form logic extracted to `checkForm.ts` (conversions) + `checkFormFields.tsx` (field components), reused by the page and the now edit-only `CheckDrawer`. The natural home for the [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target table/path field. _(PR-B2; `CheckNew.tsx`)_

**Week 4 total: 26 / 26 ✅ — complete** _(connection manager UI complete (6/6); check editor core shipped + **dry-run preview** (PR-D1) + **column profiler panel** (PR-D2) + the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** (PR #246 — closes the flat-file + Unity Catalog target inputs, making suites runnable); **check version-history drawer** (#280; backend #281 + frontend #282 — view-only, restore deferred to #283); **suite export/import UI** (PR-E1); **suite sharing panel** (PR-E2 backend + PR-E3 UI, gated on `SuiteRead.my_permission`); **GX-Cloud redesign complete (4/4)** — classified connection + check create pages, edit-only drawers, suite-route/categorized picker (PR-A1/A2/B1/B2); PR-3c polish 3/3 (Settings `extra="forbid"` shipped via the `.env`/`.env.app` split, #209); **Monaco custom-SQL editor** (ADR 0019 — custom-SQL as a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation`; backend #258 + frontend #259); **admin control centre** (#289 — workspace-admin authz + `/admin/{suites,users,access}` + the `/admin` page & 403, pulling the Week-7 prototype-adoption admin tasks forward))_

+### Execution UI (4 tasks — 4/4 ✅)

+> The roadmap's 5th Execution-UI task — **recent-runs audit table** — was **moved to Week 6** (counted there). Why: the [DataQ Design System](#week-6--results-dashboard--alerting) prototype makes the **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** (`/dashboard`, the [Week-6 task](#week-6--results-dashboard--alerting)) the page-home for the recent-runs widget, and its row drill-down target is the Week-6 routed run-detail page (`/results/:runId`, [#268](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/268) — promotes `Results.tsx`'s in-page `openRun` state to a route). - [x] ✅ Run now panel — suite picker, env / datasource, notification target — `RunNowPanel` (`components/runs/`): a **cross-suite** run launcher (modal off a **Run now** button in the Results header), distinct from the suite-detail **Run** button (#245) which runs one suite in context. A searchable suite `Select` filtered to the `owner`/`admin`/`edit` ladder the backend `POST /suites/{id}/run` enforces (view-only suites never appear); a read-only `Descriptions` readout of the picked suite's **env** (tag) + **datasource** (connection name + type) + **target** (one-line `catalog.schema.table` / flat-file `path`, mirroring `suiteTarget.ts`). A targetless suite blocks **Run** with an inline reason rather than a backend 422. On trigger it hands off to the shared `LiveRunProgress` drawer (modal closes; run watched + cancellable). **Notification target ships as a clearly-labelled disabled placeholder** ("coming in Week 6") — no notification backend exists yet (Teams/`ResultPublisher` alert routing is Week 6), so it doesn't jump the gun. Self-contained (owns its data fetch + progress drawer) so the Week-6 UI rework can drop it onto the dedicated Execution page unchanged. 3 component tests (no-runnable empty state, env/datasource/target render + trigger, targetless-suite block).

- [x] ✅ Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button — `LiveRunProgress` drawer (`components/runs/`): opens on the run id returned by the manual **Run** button (#245) instead of bouncing to `/results`, and polls `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (#301) on a self-scheduling timeout (not `setInterval` — no overlapping fetches) until the run is terminal (`succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled`), then stops. Shows the run-status tag + a completed/total `Progress` bar + a per-check list (a `Spin`+"pending" while a check's `status` is null, its severity `Tag` once resolved). An editor (`canRun`) gets a **Cancel** button (`POST /runs/{id}/cancel` #302) on a non-terminal run; cancel reflects the terminal state immediately + the next poll confirms it. On terminal, a "View full results →" link to `/results`. Body keyed by run id so opening a new run restarts polling; `pollMs` is injectable for deterministic tests. 7 component tests (pending/resolved render, poll-until-terminal-then-stop, cancel happy path, cancel hidden when terminal / non-editor, fetch-error alert). **(A3)**

- [x] ✅ Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules — `SchedulesPanel` (`components/suites/`): suite-detail card over the A7 schedules CRUD (#314) — an antd table (cron · timezone · next run · last run · status · delete) + an add form (cron `Input` + IANA-timezone `Select`, default UTC). **Pause/resume** is the `enabled` toggle (`PATCH /schedules/{id}`); **delete** is `DELETE /schedules/{id}`; **create** is `POST /schedules`. Edit-gated (`canManage` = `canRun`): viewers see a read-only `enabled`/`paused` tag, no controls — matching the backend gate. Timezone list comes from `Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone')` (no hardcoded table; UTC-first). Cron/timezone validated server-side → 422 surfaced inline. `api/schedules.ts` + 6 component tests (list, empty, create-with-UTC-default, pause-toggle, delete, viewer-read-only). **(A6)**

A targetless suite blocks **Run** with an inline reason rather than a backend 422. On trigger it hands off to the shared `LiveRunProgress` drawer (modal closes; run watched + cancellable). **Notification target ships as a clearly-labelled disabled placeholder** ("coming in Week 6") — no notification backend exists yet (Teams/`ResultPublisher` alert routing is Week 6), so it doesn't jump the gun. Self-contained (owns its data fetch + progress drawer) so the Week-6 UI rework can drop it onto the dedicated Execution page unchanged. 3 component tests (no-runnable empty state, env/datasource/target render + trigger, targetless-suite block).

- [x] ✅ Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button — `LiveRunProgress` drawer (`components/runs/`): opens on the run id returned by the manual **Run** button (#245) instead of bouncing to `/results`, and polls `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (#301) on a self-scheduling timeout (not `setInterval` — no overlapping fetches) until the run is terminal (`succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled`), then stops. Shows the run-status tag + a completed/total `Progress` bar + a per-check list (a `Spin`+"pending" while a check's `status` is null, its severity `Tag` once resolved). An editor (`canRun`) gets a **Cancel** button (`POST /runs/{id}/cancel` #302) on a non-terminal run; cancel reflects the terminal state immediately + the next poll confirms it. On terminal, a "View full results →" link to `/results`. Body keyed by run id so opening a new run restarts polling; `pollMs` is injectable for deterministic tests. 7 component tests (pending/resolved render, poll-until-terminal-then-stop, cancel happy path, cancel hidden when terminal / non-editor, fetch-error alert). **(A3)**

- [x] ✅ Scheduled runs table — create, pause, delete cron schedules — `SchedulesPanel` (`components/suites/`): suite-detail card over the A7 schedules CRUD (#314) — an antd table (cron · timezone · next run · last run · status · delete) + an add form (cron `Input` + IANA-timezone `Select`, default UTC). **Pause/resume** is the `enabled` toggle (`PATCH /schedules/{id}`); **delete** is `DELETE /schedules/{id}`; **create** is `POST /schedules`. Edit-gated (`canManage` = `canRun`): viewers see a read-only `enabled`/`paused` tag, no controls — matching the backend gate. Timezone list comes from `Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone')` (no hardcoded table; UTC-first). Cron/timezone validated server-side → 422 surfaced inline. `api/schedules.ts` + 6 component tests (list, empty, create-with-UTC-default, pause-toggle, delete, viewer-read-only). **(A6)**
-- [ ] ⬜ Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results

+- [ ] 🔵 Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results — **moved to Week 6** (counted there), folded into the Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard (`/dashboard`) where the prototype homes the recent-runs widget. Rationale in the group note above + the Week-6 task.

- [x] ✅ **Suite Triggers panel** — UI over the `trigger_bindings` CRUD (#172): bind/unbind a pipeline/DAG (`provider` + `pipeline_or_dag_id` + `env`) to a suite so it runs on the pipeline's success. **Suite-level**, not a check field (orchestration providers are never a datasource/check attribute, CLAUDE.md §4); inline `TriggersPanel` card on the suite detail — list + enable/disable toggle + remove + add form (ADF/Airflow · pipeline-or-DAG id · env), edit-gated (read-only for viewers). `api/triggerBindings.ts` + 6 component tests. _(PR #247; closes [#216](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/216))_

-### Results dashboard (10 tasks — 2/10 ✅)
+### Results dashboard (11 tasks — 2/11 ✅)

> **Build as an in-app React page** (the GX-Cloud-style redesign Phase C), **not Grafana** — reading Postgres directly would bypass DataQ's per-suite sharing + PII redaction; Grafana is deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on ([ADR 0018](adr/0018-results-surface-and-grafana-deferral.md), accepted). The Week-5 run-enablement backend it gates on is **done**: `runs.py` read endpoints (`GET /runs`, `GET /runs/{id}`, `GET /pipeline_runs`) + `POST /suites/{id}/run` (PR-C0b); [#215](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/215) target (PR-C0a). The `results.metric_value`/`duration_ms` columns were seam-built for these trend charts (ADR 0012).

- [x] ✅ **Results page scaffold** (PR-C1) — the in-app `/results` page + sidebar nav (Connections · Suites · **Results** · Profile). **Runs tab**: a runs table (suite name, status, triggered-by, started, duration) with a status filter, each row opening a **run-detail drawer** that drills into the per-check results (check name + expectation, severity tag, `metric_value`, observed value). **Pipeline runs tab**: the orchestration monitoring feed (`pipeline_runs`) with a provider filter. Pure presentation helpers (`resultsFormat.ts` — duration/timestamp formatters, status→colour maps) are unit-tested; `api/runs.ts` mirrors the C0b schemas. Demo seed (`demo_data.py`) now lands runs + results (pass/pass/warn/fail spread) + two pipeline-runs + suite targets so the page has real content. 10 vitest + 2 Playwright (`results.spec.ts`). _The rich widgets below build on this scaffold._
- [ ] ⬜ Health score stat cards + 7-day trend chart

lines: > **Design source — how to open the prototype (read before picking up any task in this track):** the claude.ai/design project **"DataQ Design System"** — `projectId` **`317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d`** (owner Arijit). Read it with the **`/design-sync`** skill / **`DesignSync`** tool over the claude.ai login: `DesignSync method=list_files projectId=…`, then `method=get_file path=<…>`. Key paths: **`readme.md`** = the canonical spec (voice · color · type · spacing · status model · component/template manifest); **`guidelines/*.card.html`** = foundation specimen cards; **`templates/app/*.jsx`** = framework-free recreations of each screen; **`components/<group>/*.jsx`** = the component recreations; **`tokens/*.css`** = raw token values; **`uploads/DESIGN.md`** = the *superseded teal* brief (ignore for palette). ⚠️ The hosted share link `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/design/h/SVkQJqee7xsc2FNWVOsnIQ` returns **404** — reach the prototype via DesignSync, not the link. The `.jsx` / `_ds_bundle.js` are **reference only** — build in real antd reading `theme.ts`.

- [ ] ⬜ **Nav / shell restructure** — add **Dashboard** as the landing entry; reorder the sider to **Dashboard · Connections · Suites · Results · Profile**; add the footer group **Settings · Admin · Documentation** (Admin/Settings land in Week 7). Change the login redirect `/` → `/dashboard` (was `/connections`). Touches `App.tsx` `NAV_ITEMS` + `<Routes>` only — the nav already prefix-matches ([#192](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/192)). _Design ref: `templates/app/AppShell.jsx` (white header + light sider + footer nav) + readme §"What's in here → Templates → `templates/app`" (the sidebar + footer item lists)._

-- [ ] ⬜ **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard page** (`/dashboard`, `DashboardScreen`) — the new landing screen that **hosts the existing Week-6 dashboard widgets** (health-score stat cards, 7-day quality-trend chart, per-suite performance bars, recent-runs table). This is the **page home** for the "Health score stat cards", "Per-suite pass/fail bars" and "Per-check historical trend chart" tasks above — build them here rather than on `/results`. **Prereq:** a chart-library decision (antd ships no charts → `@ant-design/charts` or `recharts`; runs through `pnpm audit` + bundle-size gate → ADR-light note). Render only backend-backed KPIs (see the W7 *KPI honesty pass*). _Design ref: `templates/app/DashboardScreen.jsx`; components `data/MetricCard.jsx`, `data/Card.jsx`, `feedback/ProgressBar.jsx`, `data/DataTable.jsx`; readme §"Templates" (Enhanced Monitoring — KPIs · Quality Trends · Suite Performance · Recent Runs)._

+- [ ] ⬜ **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard page** (`/dashboard`, `DashboardScreen`) — the new landing screen that **hosts the existing Week-6 dashboard widgets** (health-score stat cards, 7-day quality-trend chart, per-suite performance bars, **recent-runs table** — the task moved from Week 5). This is the **page home** for the "Health score stat cards", "Per-suite pass/fail bars", "Per-check historical trend chart" and "Recent runs audit table" tasks above — build them here rather than on `/results`. **Prereq:** a chart-library decision (antd ships no charts → `@ant-design/charts` or `recharts`; runs through `pnpm audit` + bundle-size gate → ADR-light note). Render only backend-backed KPIs (see the W7 *KPI honesty pass*). _Design ref: `templates/app/DashboardScreen.jsx`; components `data/MetricCard.jsx`, `data/Card.jsx`, `feedback/ProgressBar.jsx`, `data/DataTable.jsx`; readme §"Templates" (Enhanced Monitoring — KPIs · Quality Trends · Suite Performance · Recent Runs)._

- [ ] ⬜ **New Suite → dedicated page** (`/suites/new`, `SuiteFlow`) — extract the `SuiteDrawer` **create** path into a routed *details / source* page reusing `suiteTarget.ts` + the run-target editor (PR #246). Removes the drawer's create branch; `Suites.tsx` swaps `setDrawer({open:true})` for `navigate('/suites/new')`. _Design ref: `templates/app/SuiteFlow.jsx` (New Suite → Details/Source) → `CheckFlow.jsx` (Define Rules); readme §"Templates" full-page-flows note._

- [ ] ⬜ **Edit flows → dedicated pages** — unify edit onto the existing create pages via an `:id` route + prefill, reusing the already-extracted form specs (`connectionFormSpec.ts` / `checkForm.ts` / `suiteTarget.ts`): connection edit `/connections/:id/edit` → `ConnectionNew`; suite edit `/suites/:id/edit` → `SuiteFlow`; check edit `/suites/:id/checks/:checkId/edit` → `CheckNew`. **Removes** the `ConnectionDrawer` / `CheckDrawer` / `SuiteDrawer` **edit** drawers; the parent pages drop their `editing` / `drawer` state for `navigate()`. (Dedups with [#204](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/204).) _Design ref: `templates/app/ConnectionFlow.jsx` (Configuration), `SuiteFlow.jsx`, `CheckFlow.jsx` — the create flows; edit reuses the same page shape, prefilled._

- [ ] ⬜ **Results drill-down → dedicated route** (`/results/:runId`, `ResultsScreen` detail) — promote the in-page `openRun` `useState` ([Results.tsx:54](../frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx#L54)) to a routed run-detail page with back-nav, per-check results (severity tags + `metric_value` + observed), and CSV/JSON download. Deep-linkable + refreshable (the prototype's `ResultsScreen` flow). _Design ref: `templates/app/ResultsScreen.jsx`; readme §"Templates" (Results → click a run → Run detail with per-check results + CSV/JSON download)._

280	**Week-2 exit gate:** All six connection types configurable + testable via API, credentials in the SecretStore. — **met** (Snowflake / ADF / Airflow / ADLS Gen2 / S3 / Unity Catalog behind the `ConnectionAdapter` seam + registry; real Key Vault provisioning deferred to W7).

Live warehouse/file runs remain the deferred smoke.
282	**Completed since project start (2026-05-24):** see [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) for the per-PR ledger. Headlines:
283	- **Week 1** — governance + tooling lock (#1–#37), structlog/error-envelope/FastAPI skeleton + SQLAlchemy/Alembic baseline (PR 2), Azure AD SSO end-to-end (PR 3), async backbone + Snowflake GX adapter + run/result persistence (PR 4).
284	- **Week 2** — connection manager for all six types (Snowflake/ADF/Airflow/ADLS/S3/UC) behind the `ConnectionAdapter` seam; ADF + Airflow orchestration event receivers (secret-in-URL / HMAC) + connection adapters; re-auth endpoint; ADRs 0005/0006/0007/0010/0011/0012.
285	- **Week 3** — suite & check CRUD + sharing + export/import + dry-run; severity tiers (ADR 0005/0016) + monitor-kind seam (ADR 0012); column profiler (all 4 datasources); GX `CheckRunner`s (Snowflake/flat-file/UC) on the shared `gx_runner`; batch resolution; integration tests. Plus the testing-discipline upgrade (adversarial harness + mutation spikes, CONTRIBUTING rule 4a).
286	- **Week 4 (complete, 26/26)** — frontend: app-router shell + connections list (#191), spec-driven add-connection drawer (#196) + Snowflake key-pair (#193), connection edit/re-auth/delete (#198), suites list/detail two-panel (#200), catalog-driven check editor (#203). Plus Week-5 early-credit (worker runner-dispatch #146, ADF/Airflow 10-min polling #171, `trigger_bindings` CRUD #172, per-suite run target + dispatch ungate #215 — `Suite.target` + `run_target` resolver, `_trigger_suites` now dispatches `run_suite`; **manual run trigger + run/result read API** PR-C0b — `POST /suites/{id}/run` (edit-gated, resolves the target up front) + `api/v1/runs.py` `GET /runs`·`/runs/{id}`·`/pipeline_runs`, suite-authz-scoped, the read surface the Results page consumes) and the Python 3.13 + Snowflake 4 CVE refresh (ADR 0017, #129) + Dependabot batch (#202 pyarrow direct dep + 10 bumps). Plus the **custom-SQL check editor** (ADR 0019; backend #258 + Monaco frontend #259), the **`.env`/`.env.app` split** unblocking `Settings` `extra="forbid"` (#209), and the testing/CI hardening (frontend Stryker mutation harness #255; **CI is now an enforced merge gate** — 12 required checks on the `main` ruleset). Plus the **check version-history drawer** (#280) and the **admin control centre** (#289 — see below) closing out the week.
287	**Results surface (Phase C, done through PR-C1):** the in-app **`/results` page** shipped — runs table + run-detail drill-down (per-check results, severity tags) + orchestration pipeline-runs tab + sidebar nav (Connections · Suites · Results · Profile), on the C0b read API; ADR 0018 (in-app page over Grafana) accepted; demo seed lands runs/results/pipeline-runs. Rich dashboard widgets (health cards, trends, per-suite bars, sample-row drill-down [#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226), export) remain Week 6.

Remaining Week-5 surface: **scheduling backend (A7)**, execution UI (run-now / live-progress / scheduled-runs / recent-runs), run-history retention purge. Live warehouse/file run remains the deferred Week-1 smoke.
